zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Jane de Roll於1490年出生在英國蘇格蘭艾斯爾時，她的父親Sir John de Roll30歲，母親Elizabeth Brownfield20歲。她於1509年與Thomas Corry deKelwood在蘇格蘭艾斯爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1530年在蘇格蘭艾斯爾享年40歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Roll
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German (also Röll): from Middle High German rolle, rulle ‘roll, list’, possibly applied as a metonymic occupational name for a scribe.
German: from a short form of the personal names   Rudolf   or   Roland  .
German: habitational name from either of two places called Rolle, in Westphalia and Pomerania.

variants.name: Rowles
meanings: English (Gloucestershire and Oxfordshire): of Norman origin, a variant of Roll with genitive or post-medieval excrescent -s. See Rolfe .

variants.name: Roelle
meanings: German (Rölle): variant of Röll (see Roll 1). Compare Rolle 2.

variants.name: Rudolf
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Heinz, Bernhard, Erna, Gottlieb, Helmuth, Johann, Manfred, Theodor, Uwe.
German, Slovenian, Czech, Polish, Croatian, and Hungarian: from the ancient Germanic personal name Rudolf, composed of Old High German hrōd ‘renown’ + wolf ‘wolf’.

variants.name: Rowell
meanings: English:

habitational name principally from Rothwell (Yorkshire), but Rothwell in Lincolnshire and Northamptonshire also gave rise to surnames. The placenames derive from Old English roth ‘clearing’ + wella ‘well, spring, stream’.
habitational name from Rowell in Chudleigh (Devon), named with Old English rūh ‘rough’ + hyll ‘hill’. The name may have been confused with Roll .

variants.name: Roell
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Eberhard, Hans.
German and Swiss German (Röll): see Roll 1.

variants.name: Rolle
meanings: German and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Roll  .
German (Rölle): variant of Röll (see Roll 1). Compare Roelle .
Germanized form of Sorbian Rola: nickname (or a topographic name) for a farmer, from rola ‘cultivated field’.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jaine
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jane .

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Jane de Roll was born in 1490, in Ayrshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, her father, Sir John de Roll, was 30 and her mother, Elizabeth Brownfield, was 20. She married Thomas Corry deKelwood in 1509, in Ayrshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1530, in Ayrshire, Scotland, at the age of 40.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Jane de Roll est née en 1490, à Ayrshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir John de Roll, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Brownfield, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Corry deKelwood en 1509, à Ayrshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1530, à Ayrshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 40 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Jane de Roll 1490 in Ayrshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John de Roll 30 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Brownfield 20. Sie hat 1509 in Ayrshire, Scotland Thomas Corry deKelwood geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1530 im Alter von 40 Jahren in Ayrshire, Scotland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Jane de Roll è nata il nel 1490 nel Ayrshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, sua padre, Sir John de Roll, aveva 30 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Brownfield, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Corry deKelwood nel 1509, nel Ayrshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1530, nel Ayrshire, Scotland, all'età di 40 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Jane de Roll(이)가 1490년 영국 스코틀랜드 에이르실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John de Roll(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Brownfield(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1509년 스컷랜드 에이르실르에서 Thomas Corry deKelwood(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1530년 스컷랜드 에이르실르에서 40세의 나이로 사망했다.
